summary: Recent updates to Firefox's image decoders focused on improving performance and efficiency in handling images. By caching the specification hash on `nsIURI`, a component that manages URLs, Firefox can now retrieve image data more quickly when loading and rendering images on web pages. This change helps to reduce loading times and improve the overall browsing experience, especially for image-heavy sites. While the technical adjustments may not have any _immediate visible change_ for users, they contribute to a smoother and faster interaction with online content.
